tweets.text:
Paradigm shift: Since crypto was founded in 2009, it’s been outperforming absolutely every other asset class.

Until this cycle, where the tables turned, and equities + commodities + venture capital + private equity outperformed it (including its best individual performers, like hyperliquid).

While it may seem like a temporary slump, the psychological shift in liquidity will be permanent: there will be pockets of fundamentals that outperform from within crypto, but as a whole, previous levels of parabola will be hindered.

tweets.text:
The divergence itself is not meaningful (yet) but higher high on DXY into headlines that marked a higher low on EURUSD 

Now if this accepts above today's high then the divergence is invalidated - but if DXY breaks and closes below range lows from here, I'm pretty confident that's a local top

This is currently the one primary missing piece from the post-Covid reflationary boom analog - and whilst I remain bullish DXY into 2027, I think this range below 100 is distribution not re-accumulation (at present) - above 101 we can get excited about a new dollar bull market  

And if yields have peaked locally and inflation breakevens are bottoming locally (which homebuilders seem to be convinced of) -> real rates lower -> should be a headwind for DXY

tweets.text:
Gm ☕️

Spot bids refilled here overnight above 90d rvwap ~73.3 

Discretionary not systematic — plan from the very start has been bid mid 60s -> sell low 80s as avwap from aths at 83.7 holds on first attempt -> bid higher low at 90d rvwap -> second attempt = breakout and bull market confirmation (https://t.co/Jia946itvg)

So far so good… now we see if risk on summer extends to crypto 

People getting bullish where it’s most favourable to be bearish and bearish where it’s most favourable to be bullish  

Will share a proper update on the system shortly but it’s very mixed — still long majors but short BTC so technically long altcoin dominance, though I expect it will close out the long majors soon and mark an L for this recent trade

Macro models are also pointing towards some interesting recommendations for June…
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
4 weeks later here we are

Selling the spot I bought for the bounce in the 60s — trade was buy 65-68, sell 83-84 and that’s done here 

Systematic is still very much long and seems to think we’re gonna break this on the first attempt 👀 https://t.co/ATlorpHWFn

tweets.text:
Appreciate the more neutral coverage by Reuters and Bloomberg on $XFAB today.

Although it would be nicer to focus more on the structural thesis presented…

Around 800 vdc power semis $NVDA exposure + with ongoing $NVDA / $NOK evaluations for photonics.

And around CHIPS Act semiconductor sovereignty as the near term catalyst.

Rather than around volatility from novel information synthesis. 

I spend a lot of time looking at regulatory filings to find compelling things market missed you know…
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
$XFAB (photonics + power semis) is an interesting long idea at $1.28B MC, that I took positions in.

Given EU CHIPS act 2 is today as the catalyst for European photonics players.

> 800 VDC power semi exposure to $NVDA push through $NVTS + $POWI

> Silicon Photonics / CPO https://t.co/AZFFLbQk8O

And around CHIPS Act semiconductor sovereignty as the near term catalyst.

Rather than around volatility from novel information synthesis. 

I spend a lot of time looking at regulatory filings to find compelling things market missed you know…

tweets.text:
$MRVL earnings were a bullish indicator on the broader CPO theme (and $SIVE as the likely laser supplier).

- “Scale-up interconnect represents one of the newest and most strategically important opportunities emerging in AI infrastructure.” 

CPO thematically go brrr 

- Confirmation Celestial was selected by T1 hyperscaler for scale up.

I’ve found Celestial $AMZN warrants in the past)… so probably Amazon.

- Scale-up optics revenue next year should be more than 2x prior ~$150M outlook with Celestial

Forward revenue ramp expectations go brrr. 

- Celestial team plus $MRVL optics team was a “home run”.

Marvell sees celestial as growth vector, upstream celestial suppliers go brr

- $MRVL is now focused on bringing Celestial to high volume manufacturing. 

Volume ramp indicator

If you don’t recall, there was OSINT mapping $SIVE directly to Celestial, not through $POET.

So Celestial forward growth is a volume ramp indicator for Sivers lasers.

tweets.text:
this is exactly why  TCG's will continue to move higher 

onchain TCG platforms mint profits through gacha buybacks and marketplace fees, then recycle those fees to pull even more physical stock onto the chain.

the only way these platforms scale inventory is by  buying on the secondary market andthe most liquid marketplace (@Collector_Crypt ) ultimately wins.

It’s a game theory loop of endless upward pressure on tcg prices,  all while these platforms keep stacking revenue and profits, so for all the ppl whinging about wanting the buybacks for the token, that should come later imo, what we really want is for them to keep investing in inventory

tweets.text:
Yeah AI is making DeFi exploits more common.

No you do not need to exit all DeFi. You just need to be more selective with where you entrust your money.

The contract on the left will never be exploited, no matter how powerful an AI you point at it.

The contract on the right is inherently vulnerable, only ever as safe as the owner's private key.

This is obviously an oversimplification and protocols will necessarily be more complex and harder to review at a glance, but the difference embodies one of the biggest issues we see these days, and the root cause behind most of the exploits of the past few months: centralization risk that lands in the wrong hands.

Ethereum was built to be trustless, and should adhere to that wherever possible.

Evaluate the code. Evaluate the trust assumptions. Evaluate your own opsec.

DeFi is a spectrum - some code will be insecure, some teams will be insecure. You don't have to give up DeFi, you just need to do proper due diligence.
